topic: LLM Beliefs — an LLM's context-dependent internal tendency to treat a proposition/hypothesis/world-state as more or less likely (belief is not fact or knowledge: it can be false or uncertain)

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: The classic framing-effect belief shift (gain vs loss wording over extensionally-equivalent outcomes) is transferred from low-stakes survey judgments into high-stakes medical triage credence, holding evidence constant.
behaviors.statement: Equivalent positive vs negative framing of identical clinical evidence (e.g. '98% survival' vs '2% mortality') shifts the model's credence in a diagnosis or treatment success in high-stakes medical prompts.

five_bars.real: Framing effects are robust in human judgment and likely interact with LLM next-token priors.
five_bars.nonobvious: Medical prompts are usually benchmarked for accuracy, not latent credence under semantically-equivalent wording.
five_bars.specific: Trigger is survival/lives-saved vs mortality/lives-lost wording with identical statistics.
five_bars.robust: Testable across diagnoses, treatment recommendations, elicitation formats, and templates.
five_bars.tractable: Requires only paired prompt construction and credence extraction.

impact.rationale: Medical framing effects matter as LLMs enter clinical support/health info, but the behavioral phenomenon is already active in the literature, so impact comes mainly from mechanism and mitigation.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A distributed valence/framing direction in the mid-to-late residual stream causally biases downstream probability readout toward higher or lower medical credence under equivalent evidence; activation patching between frame variants on matched cases localizes it.
mechanisms.boring_null: The model may merely paraphrase user wording in its answer style while internal credence is unchanged; or differences come from extraction/decoding noise.

data.source: Paired clinical vignettes with mathematically equivalent gain/loss framing and direct credence queries
data.note: Balance disease prevalence, treatment efficacy, and lexical overlap across frames.

novelty.closest: This Treatment Works, Right? (arXiv:2604.05051); CogBias; CoBRA
novelty.differentiation: Delta is a causal valence/framing residual direction that can be patched to move probabilities, not another behavioral framing demonstration.
novelty.caveat: Medical framing is already well-explored behaviorally; moderate unless the causal account is unusually precise.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Applying causal scrubbing or linear concept editing to suppress the framing/valence direction makes medical credence frame-invariant while preserving task competence on unframed questions.
mechanisms.boring_null: Any edit that reduces verbosity or hedging may superficially reduce measured framing; or framing may be diffuse and not editable.

data.source: Existing clinical QA sets augmented with matched framed variants
data.note: Use held-out diseases to test edit generalization.

novelty.closest: Subspace suppression / concept-erasure for bias mitigation + prior framing papers
novelty.differentiation: Suppresses a framing subspace to produce frame-invariant clinical judgments.
novelty.caveat: Mechanistically-targeted debiasing via subspace editing is now a common template.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Frame wording affects belief through a small set of lexical-valence and risk-description features that mediate between prompt interpretation and explicit probability generation; a path-specific account quantifies this vs evidence-token pathways.
mechanisms.boring_null: A complete account may fail if many weak pathways contribute; or frame effects may be shallow prompt-template artifacts rather than a coherent circuit.

data.source: Synthetic but realistic medical notes with controlled frame manipulations
data.note: Designed for dense causal tracing rather than benchmark breadth.

novelty.closest: Behavioral framing papers + generic circuit-tracing on sentiment/uncertainty features
novelty.differentiation: Traces the path from frame tokens through valence/uncertainty features to final credence, beyond outcome-level reports.
novelty.caveat: The path may collapse into familiar sentiment effects unless tied tightly to belief readout.

mechanisms.hypothesis: An interpretable frame-valence variable explains a substantial share of otherwise-unexplained variation in medical belief statements under evidence-equivalent prompts.
mechanisms.boring_null: Probe success may reflect linearly-decodable output style rather than causal internal state; or evidence features alone explain nearly all variance.

data.source: Calibration-style confidence-elicitation datasets plus newly framed medical variants
data.note: Use separate train/test prompt families.

novelty.closest: Standard probing analyses in framing/sentiment studies
novelty.differentiation: Shows a frame-valence variable explains credence variance, a compact summary.
novelty.caveat: Probe-based variance explanation alone is weakly novel and vulnerable to correlational overclaiming.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Small frame-driven credence shifts near decision thresholds causally produce clinically-meaningful recommendation reversals (treatment/referral/urgency).
mechanisms.boring_null: Credence shifts may be too small to affect final decisions; or safety fine-tuning may already neutralize framing in recommendations even if stated probabilities shift.

data.source: Existing medical triage scenarios with threshold-based decisions
data.note: Focus on edge cases near treatment or referral cutoffs.

novelty.closest: Existing work on framing-induced recommendation shifts in medical LLM evals
novelty.differentiation: Ties framing to threshold crossings/treatment reversals — a more operational harm metric than average probability shifts.
novelty.caveat: Close to existing behavioral evaluation unless paired with new mechanistic predictors.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: The conjunction-fallacy / representativeness heuristic (a detailed conjunction judged more probable than its broader constituent) is transferred from toy personality descriptions into legal-evidence credence, where a vivid guilt story competes with a logically broader hypothesis.
behaviors.statement: In legal reasoning prompts, a vivid detailed guilt narrative can receive higher credence than a logically more probable broader hypothesis given the same evidence.

five_bars.real: Representativeness effects are classic and legal prompts naturally invite narrative reasoning.
five_bars.nonobvious: Most legal-LLM evaluation targets argument quality / verdict accuracy, not probability coherence under nested hypotheses.
five_bars.specific: Trigger is a descriptive suspect profile plus two options where one is a conjunction/subtype of the other.
five_bars.robust: Vary crime types, evidence strengths, and wording of nested hypotheses.
five_bars.tractable: Needs paired legal vignettes with explicit probability comparison or numerical credence elicitation.

impact.rationale: A legal-domain conjunction fallacy would matter for bail, probable-cause, and procedural recommendations, and a mechanistic account of narrative-over-breadth reasoning could influence legal-AI evaluation and general probabilistic-reasoning research.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A representativeness-like circuit encoding profile-to-story match causally boosts credence for specific legal narratives even when a broader hypothesis dominates probabilistically; intervening on attention to descriptive-profile tokens vs logic markers moves the effect.
mechanisms.boring_null: The model may misunderstand nested hypotheses due to wording ambiguity rather than exhibit a true belief distortion; or differences reflect response-formatting heuristics.

data.source: Legalized conjunction-fallacy vignettes with explicit nested hypotheses and evidence controls
data.note: Include logic-check annotations for the validity of the nesting.

novelty.closest: Will the Real Linda...; Token Bias; Dual Traits in Probabilistic Reasoning
novelty.differentiation: Prior work is behavioral / generic-reasoning; a causal representativeness circuit in legal narratives with interventions restoring monotonicity is a substantive advance.
novelty.caveat: Must show genuine legal-domain transfer, not a thin re-skin of Linda-style prompts.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Editing or steering toward logic-consistency features associated with subset relations makes legal credence respect monotonicity while preserving narrative-summarization ability.
mechanisms.boring_null: Generic chain-of-thought encouragement might fix the issue without any localized representativeness mechanism; or editing may damage fluency and indirectly change credence.

data.source: Logic-consistency datasets combined with legal narrative prompts
data.note: Train edit on non-legal nested events, evaluate transfer to legal domain.

novelty.closest: Representation editing / steering for logical consistency and reasoning repair
novelty.differentiation: Steers logical-inclusion features to recover monotonicity — an actionable mitigation targeted at legal conjunction errors.
novelty.caveat: Editing-based correction is familiar; hinges on features being specific and generalizing beyond the template.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Final legal belief is produced by competition between a story-coherence pathway and a symbolic/structural inclusion pathway, with the former dominating under vivid suspect descriptions; quantify which path dominates.
mechanisms.boring_null: There may be no separable pathways (signals entangled throughout); or prompting may not reliably induce conjunction errors in frontier models.

data.source: Minimal-pair legal stories with controlled vividness and explicit logical structure
data.note: Optimized for circuit tracing over benchmark breadth.

novelty.closest: Dual-process accounts in LLM probabilistic reasoning (Dual Traits...)
novelty.differentiation: A dual-path narrative-coherence vs symbolic-inclusion competition instantiated mechanistically, not just behaviorally.
novelty.caveat: Dual-path stories are already in the air; burden is on clear causal dissociation.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Cases of legal conjunction errors show high narrative-coherence activation and weak logical-breadth activation before answer generation; sparse probes for each visualize case clusters by mechanism dominance.
mechanisms.boring_null: Probe variables may not correspond to meaningful internal concepts; or a single generic confidence feature could explain both without distinct mechanisms.

data.source: Legal reasoning corpora plus synthetic nested-event prompts
data.note: Use held-out crime categories for explanation validity.

novelty.closest: Standard probing of narrative/logical reasoning features
novelty.differentiation: Shows narrative-coherence and logical-breadth signals jointly predict conjunction errors — a compact diagnostic decomposition.
novelty.caveat: Probe-predictive diagnostics are relatively incremental without intervention.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Narrative-driven belief inflation transfers into legal decision thresholds, increasing punitive or overconfident recommendations (bail, probable-cause, investigative priority); explicit monotonicity checks mitigate.
mechanisms.boring_null: Models may hedge enough that downstream legal recommendations remain unchanged; or legal safety tuning may dampen decisive outputs despite underlying distortions.

data.source: Thresholded legal decision prompts derived from nested-hypothesis scenarios
data.note: Measure recommendation reversals under equivalent evidence.

novelty.closest: Behavioral work on legal-reasoning bias + probabilistic errors affecting recommendations
novelty.differentiation: Connects conjunction overcredence directly to biased bail/probable-cause recommendations — domain-specific practical salience.
novelty.caveat: As a consequence analysis it is useful but not deeply novel unless mechanistically grounded.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: The textual misinformation/continued-influence effect is checked in vision-language models: whether a misleading caption or preamble shifts credence about image content even when visual evidence weakly contradicts it.
behaviors.statement: In VL models, a misleading textual caption or preamble can shift credence about what is present in an image even when visual evidence weakly contradicts it.

five_bars.real: Multimodal models fuse text and image, so text-induced interpretation distortions are plausible.
five_bars.nonobvious: VL benchmarks emphasize accuracy on visible objects, not latent image beliefs under caption contamination.
five_bars.specific: Trigger is exposure to a false caption before answering probabilistic questions about image contents.
five_bars.robust: Vary object ambiguity, caption confidence, correction timing, and image quality.
five_bars.tractable: Existing VQA/captioning images can be repurposed with synthetic misleading captions.

impact.rationale: Misleading captions undermining visual-grounded belief materially affects safety-critical multimodal use; robustness, interpretability, and evaluation researchers would build on it.

behaviors.notes: Distinct from B7: B6 is misleading semantic CONTENT (false caption) conflicting with weak visual evidence; B7 is an authority/POLISH halo with identical evidential content (per dedup pass).

mechanisms.hypothesis: A text-dominant cross-modal integration circuit causes false-caption tokens to override ambiguous visual evidence, raising belief in non-present content; patching visual activations from clean runs into miscaptioned runs causally rescues the judgment.
mechanisms.boring_null: The model may simply comply with the user text when uncertain rather than internally believing the caption; effect may vanish on high-resolution/unambiguous images.

data.source: COCO/VQAv2 images with misleading captions and direct object-probability queries
data.note: Stratify by image ambiguity and object salience.

novelty.closest: How VLMs Process Conflicting Information Across Modalities (arXiv:2507.01790)
novelty.differentiation: Emphasizes weak-evidence override plus patch-level visual interventions, but the core text-over-vision circuit and layerwise override are already established.
novelty.caveat: Very close prior art; minimal novelty unless the rescue intervention exposes a genuinely new causal locus.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Steering/editing layers to upweight vision-derived evidence over prefatory text during belief formation makes image credence more caption-robust without harming OCR/VQA.
mechanisms.boring_null: Simple system prompting to trust the image could explain gains without identifying a mechanism; edits may reduce language conditioning broadly and hurt instruction following.

data.source: Balanced clean/miscaptioned image-question pairs
data.note: Use held-out object categories for edit evaluation.

novelty.closest: Pixels Versus Priors (arXiv:2505.17127)
novelty.differentiation: Robustness via steering/editing to upweight visual grounding is more intervention-centric than prior descriptive conflict studies.
novelty.caveat: Many groups will view it as an expected extension unless transfer/generalization is strong.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Belief distortion arises when caption tokens seed an early scene hypothesis that later visual processing confirms rather than independently evaluates; map the full route from caption tokens through cross-attention heads into latent scene summary.
mechanisms.boring_null: No concise route may exist if text and vision are deeply entangled across many layers; misleading captions may only affect answer wording, not scene representation.

data.source: Small hand-curated ambiguous-image set with token-level and region-level annotations
data.note: Chosen for detailed circuit tracing.

novelty.closest: How VLMs Process Conflicting Information Across Modalities (arXiv:2507.01790)
novelty.differentiation: Slightly more specific routing account (caption-token cross-attention into a scene-summary variable) but conceptually overlaps heavily with existing layerwise analyses.
novelty.caveat: Needs sharper causal specificity than existing head/layer analyses to avoid being derivative.

mechanisms.hypothesis: VL belief under caption contamination is well explained by a latent text-prior vs visual-evidence tradeoff variable measurable before decoding.
mechanisms.boring_null: Simple image-ambiguity scores alone may explain the effect; probe variables may reflect post-hoc answer tendencies rather than pre-answer belief.

data.source: VQA uncertainty datasets plus synthetic misleading-caption variants
data.note: Include calibrated human ambiguity ratings when available.

novelty.closest: Do VLMs See or Guess? (arXiv:2606.10400)
novelty.differentiation: A probe for an explicit text-prior vs visual-evidence tradeoff variable is somewhat more representationally explicit than prior behavioral/layerwise studies.
novelty.caveat: Close to established 'priors vs pixels' framing; depends on finding a reusable latent variable with predictive power.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Text contamination can cause VL models to cross decision thresholds in image-based high-stakes tasks (e.g. radiology-like triage, document fraud review) by inflating belief in text-suggested findings; abstention/visual-grounding checks mitigate.
mechanisms.boring_null: Specialized medical/document models may rely little on free-form captions; decision layers may be robust even if descriptive credence shifts.

data.source: Document VQA and public medical-image QA sets with injected misleading textual context
data.note: Focus on tasks where textual metadata commonly accompanies images.

novelty.closest: Words or Vision: Blind Faith in Text (arXiv:2503.02199)
novelty.differentiation: Links caption-induced credence shifts to crossing high-stakes decision thresholds — a deployment-relevant decision-theoretic angle absent from most prior mechanistic papers.
novelty.caveat: Less mechanistically deep unless threshold crossing is tied to a causal internal variable.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: The source-credibility / presentation-halo effect (attractive or authoritative presentation raises perceived credibility) is transferred to VL models: manipulating visual presentation quality/professionalism of a document/image source while keeping factual content fixed.
behaviors.statement: In VL models, authoritative or polished visual presentation of a source/document increases internal credence in an image-linked proposition even when the underlying evidential content is unchanged.

five_bars.real: VL systems plausibly use layout/style cues as reliability proxies; presentation is easy to A/B test.
five_bars.nonobvious: A belief halo from presentation is distinct from OCR/extraction accuracy.
five_bars.specific: Trigger is identical evidence rendered in polished/authoritative vs plain presentation.
five_bars.robust: Vary layout, seals, branding, document type, and model family.
five_bars.tractable: Plausibly a localizable source-authority direction feeding belief readout.

impact.rationale: Presentation-driven credence halos could distort multimodal decision-making in education, journalism, and safety review; bias and mechanistic-interpretability communities would care.

behaviors.notes: Distinct from B6 (semantic caption misinformation): B7 holds evidence fixed and varies only presentation polish/authority (per dedup pass).

mechanisms.hypothesis: A multimodal residual-stream direction encoding source authority/polish adds to a proposition-credence direction during image-text fusion, causally raising belief when high-authority visual cues are present.
mechanisms.boring_null: The model may have a shallow response prior that polished documents are followed by 'yes'/higher-confidence wording, without any reusable internal credence variable.

data.source: Counterfactual document/image pairs with identical text+image evidence but manipulated layout quality, seals, badges, typography, and branding
data.note: Matched semantics and OCR content across presentation conditions.

novelty.closest: Pathological Truth Bias in VLMs (arXiv:2509.22674)
novelty.differentiation: A residual-direction account for source-authority/polish influencing credence is more mechanistic and more specific to presentation halo than prior authority-bias demonstrations.
novelty.caveat: Authority effects are known; hinges on isolating a clean causal direction rather than redescribing bias.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Editing or activation-steering an authority-related direction at multimodal layers causally increases or decreases belief reports about image propositions without altering extracted visual facts.
mechanisms.boring_null: Interventions may only change verbal hedging style or calibration tokens rather than latent belief about the proposition.

data.source: Activation steering and rank-one editing applied to VL QA on authority-manipulated prompts
data.note: Measure both answer choice and confidence/odds outputs.

novelty.closest: VLMs are Biased (arXiv:2505.23941)
novelty.differentiation: Steering an authority direction to shift belief while preserving extracted facts causally separates evidence extraction from halo-induced credence.
novelty.caveat: Could collapse into a standard steering result unless invariance of factual extraction is convincingly shown.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A sparse circuit of OCR/source-cue features, document-style features, and proposition-evidence features accounts for the halo by summing source-authority evidence into the final belief variable before answer generation.
mechanisms.boring_null: The effect could be distributed broadly across many weak features, making any compact circuit explanation misleadingly incomplete.

data.source: Synthetic scanned reports, screenshots, slides, and social posts with controlled source cues plus image-question pairs
data.note: Use SAE features over both visual and text token streams.

novelty.closest: Pathological Truth Bias in VLMs (arXiv:2509.22674)
novelty.differentiation: A sparse circuit where OCR/source cues and style features add into a belief variable is more granular than prior high-level authority-bias observations.
novelty.caveat: Needs real sparsity/causal evidence, not just correlational feature co-activation.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Specific cross-attention heads route visual source-credential tokens (logos, seals, clean report templates) into later language-side belief readout components, explaining why presentation cues transfer into proposition credence.
mechanisms.boring_null: Heads might attend to these cues only because they are salient/frequent, with no privileged role in belief formation.

data.source: Attention patching and path attribution on matched visual layouts
data.note: Focus on answer-logit and confidence changes under head ablations.

novelty.closest: Pathological Truth Bias in VLMs (arXiv:2509.22674)
novelty.differentiation: Cross-attention routing from credential tokens into belief readout is a concrete implementation hypothesis for authority effects.
novelty.caveat: Moderate overlap with generic multimodal routing analyses.

mechanisms.hypothesis: An estimated authority-sensitivity direction predicts when image-belief judgments will be miscalibrated under presentation shifts, enabling abstention or debiasing when source polish is overweighted.
mechanisms.boring_null: Simple output-level confidence recalibration may explain gains without any need for an internal authority-sensitive representation.

data.source: Reliability diagrams and OOD source-style shifts on VL proposition verification
data.note: Compare latent-direction warning signals to plain entropy/confidence baselines.

novelty.closest: VLMs are Biased (arXiv:2505.23941)
novelty.differentiation: Uses an authority-sensitivity direction as a predictor of miscalibration/abstention — more operational and selective than prior bias measurement.
novelty.caveat: Mainly an audit/application extension unless the direction is robustly reusable across datasets and model families.

behaviors.discovery_strategy_detail: Reuses linear truthfulness / truth-direction probing methods, but targets belief as context-dependent CREDENCE rather than factual truth: whether a proposition-general linear direction for current stance can be read out and steered independently of proposition identity, including for false/uncertain propositions.
behaviors.statement: LLMs may encode a reusable linear belief/credence direction such that prompts raising or lowering belief in many unrelated propositions shift activations along a common axis even when the propositions are false or uncertain.

five_bars.real: Credence manipulations measurably move activations; linear probes are standard.
five_bars.nonobvious: A proposition-agnostic credence axis separable from truth and confidence-style is non-trivial.
five_bars.specific: Trigger is the same propositions under graded credence manipulations; read projection onto a shared axis.
five_bars.robust: Test across domains, true/false/uncertain propositions, and model families.
five_bars.tractable: Linear probing plus steering directly operationalize it.

impact.rationale: A reusable proposition-agnostic internal credence axis would be a major organizing principle for belief representation in LLMs, heavily built on by interpretability, calibration, and control work.

behaviors.notes: Distinct from B10: B8 = a shared linear credence axis (geometry); B10 = additive, order-sensitive update dynamics of a low-dim state. B8 may be a component/special case of B10 (per dedup pass).

mechanisms.hypothesis: A residual-stream direction encodes proposition-agnostic current credence, separable from proposition identity and partially separable from generic confidence style, with larger projection predicting higher odds assigned to the proposition.
mechanisms.boring_null: A linear probe may exploit superficial answer-format cues or lexical certainty markers rather than a genuine internal belief variable.

data.source: Balanced proposition sets with explicit credence manipulations via evidence snippets, retractions, and ambiguity controls
data.note: Same proposition under multiple credence states with matched wording.

novelty.closest: Geometry of Truth (arXiv:2310.06824)
novelty.differentiation: Key delta is graded context-dependent credence for false/uncertain propositions, separable from proposition identity and confidence style, rather than a binary truth direction.
novelty.caveat: Sits close to truth-direction geometry; separation tests must be exceptionally strong.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Activation steering along the learned belief direction raises or lowers reported credence across many propositions while preserving proposition content and leaving generic verbosity mostly unchanged.
mechanisms.boring_null: Steering may simply bias toward affirmative answers or stronger language regardless of underlying credence.

data.source: Truth-direction steering protocols repurposed for proposition-specific credence elicitation
data.note: Include both true and false propositions and numerical probability outputs.

novelty.closest: Testing the Limits of Truth Directions (arXiv:2604.03754)
novelty.differentiation: Cross-proposition belief steering beyond truth labels toward graded credence extends established causal truth-direction interventions.
novelty.caveat: Without strong proposition-agnostic transfer and false-belief cases, reviewers will see it as incremental.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A compact account explains current credence as the sum of proposition-evidence features projected into a low-dimensional belief subspace, with dedicated retraction-sensitive features subtracting from the same variable.
mechanisms.boring_null: Belief may instead be a highly proposition-specific distributed code that only appears low-dimensional on the constructed dataset.

data.source: Multi-domain proposition/evidence datasets with graded support, contradiction, and irrelevant-evidence controls
data.note: Fit sparse feature regressions to internal belief readouts.

novelty.closest: How Context Shapes Truth (arXiv:2601.06599)
novelty.differentiation: Models credence as evidence features projected into a low-dim belief subspace with retraction subtraction — more dynamical/graded than prior context-shaped truth geometry.
novelty.caveat: Adjacent to existing geometric accounts; needs cleaner factorization.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Belief is computed in mid layers and later translated into verbal probabilities by a separate verbalization direction, explaining dissociations between internal credence and surface calibration wording.
mechanisms.boring_null: Any mid-to-late transition may simply reflect normal answer planning rather than a distinct latent belief-to-language conversion stage.

data.source: Layerwise probing and causal tracing during probability-report generation
data.note: Contrast direct yes/no, odds, and explanation formats.

novelty.closest: Geometry of Truth (arXiv:2310.06824)
novelty.differentiation: Dissociating a mid-layer belief readout from a late-layer verbalization direction is a fresher mechanistic claim not directly established by prior truth-direction work.
novelty.caveat: Must rule out simple early-vs-late confidence formatting effects.

mechanisms.hypothesis: The gap between internal belief-direction magnitude and emitted numerical probability predicts miscalibration, flagging when stated confidence is decoupled from latent stance.
mechanisms.boring_null: Simple consistency checks across paraphrased outputs might predict miscalibration just as well without accessing an internal belief representation.

data.source: Calibration benchmarks augmented with repeated belief elicitation under paraphrase and answer-format variation
data.note: Evaluate whether latent readout improves Brier/ECE risk prediction.

novelty.closest: How Context Shapes Truth (arXiv:2601.06599)
novelty.differentiation: Uses the internal-belief vs emitted-probability gap as a miscalibration predictor — a practical latent-observable discrepancy angle.
novelty.caveat: Partly an application of the main direction claim rather than an independent mechanism.
